intro:
There is no information found about a plant named Montjolya bullata. However, I found that you may be referring to Montanoa bullata, also known as Bullate Mountain Mahogany. 

Bullate Mountain Mahogany is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and may also aid in wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Bullate Mountain Mahogany include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and immune function.
